title: EchoLeak and the Emergence of CVEs for AI

questions.question: Which best describes EchoLeak (CVE-2025-32711)?
options: A jailbreak via long-context shot stuffing
A supply-chain attack on PyPI
A hardware vulnerability in NPUs

A zero-click AI command-injection flaw in Microsoft 365 Copilot (CVE-2025-32711, CVSS 9.3) causing unauthorized network information disclosure, with exfiltration via a CSP allowlisted/trusted Microsoft domain
questions.question_zh: 下列哪一项最贴切地描述了 EchoLeak（CVE-2025-32711）？
options_zh: 通过长上下文 shot stuffing 实现的 jailbreak
针对 PyPI 的供应链攻击
NPU 中的硬件漏洞
Microsoft 365 Copilot 中的零点击 AI 命令注入漏洞（CVE-2025-32711，CVSS 9.3），可造成未授权的网络信息泄露，外发通道借助 CSP 允许列表中的可信 Microsoft 域名

questions.question: What makes EchoLeak 'zero-click'?
options: The victim does not have to open the email; Copilot retrieves it as RAG context during routine queries and the injected instructions execute
It requires the user to click a link in a Teams chat
It runs in the operating-system kernel
It requires three clicks but no typing
options_zh: 受害者无需打开邮件；Copilot 在常规查询中通过 RAG 把它作为上下文检索进来，注入的指令就被执行
需要用户在 Teams 聊天里点一个链接
在操作系统内核里运行
需要点击三次但不需要打字

questions.question: Aim Labs frames EchoLeak as 'LLM Scope Violation'. What three boundaries must each be controlled independently?
options: Retrieval, scope, output
Network, disk, memory
Input, model, GPU
Prompt, response, log
questions.question_zh: Aim Labs 把 EchoLeak 描述为 “LLM Scope Violation”。需要分别独立控制的三条边界是什么？
options_zh: Retrieval、Scope、Output
网络、磁盘、内存
Input、Model、GPU
Prompt、Response、Log

questions.question: How did Microsoft/GitHub fix CamoLeak (CVSS 9.6, GitHub Copilot Chat)?
options: Added an XPIA prompt-injection filter
Disabled image rendering entirely in Copilot Chat (cost: usability) because the alternative attack surface could not be bounded
Switched the model to Llama Guard
Increased context size
questions.question_zh: Microsoft / GitHub 是怎么修复 CamoLeak（CVSS 9.6，GitHub Copilot Chat）的？
options_zh: 增加了一个 XPIA 的 prompt-injection 过滤器
完全在 Copilot Chat 中关闭了图像渲染（代价是可用性），因为另一种攻击面无法被有效约束
把模型换成了 Llama Guard
增大了 context size

questions.question: What pattern in severity calibration did the EchoLeak disclosure reveal?
options: Vendors over-rated the initial CVSS score

Vendors initially rated EchoLeak low (information disclosure); Aim Labs's MFA-code exfiltration proof-of-concept escalated the rating to 9.3 — defenders must push for comprehensive PoC for AI-specific vulnerabilities
All AI CVEs are automatically rated 10.0
Severity ratings are decided by OWASP only
questions.question_zh: EchoLeak 的披露暴露出在严重性评级上的什么模式？
options_zh: 厂商最初给出的 CVSS 分数偏高
厂商最初把 EchoLeak 评级为 “信息泄露” 类的低危；Aim Labs 给出的 MFA 验证码外发 PoC 把评级抬升到了 9.3——防御方需要主动推动给出更全面的 PoC，特别是 AI 特有漏洞
所有 AI CVE 都会自动评为 10.0
严重性评级只由 OWASP 决定

questions.question: Where do NIST and OWASP rank prompt injection as of 2024/2025?
options: NIST AI SPD 2024 frames prompt injection as a major information-security risk for generative AI; OWASP 2025 ranks it LLM01 (top application-layer threat)
NIST: outside scope; OWASP: not listed
NIST: minor; OWASP: bottom of list
Both call it a Tier-2 risk only
questions.question_zh: 截至 2024/2025 年，NIST 和 OWASP 把 prompt injection 排在什么位置？
options_zh: NIST AI SPD 2024 把 prompt injection 视为生成式 AI 的主要信息安全风险；OWASP 2025 把它列为 LLM01（应用层最高威胁）
NIST：不在范围内；OWASP：未列入
NIST：次要；OWASP：列表末尾
两者都只视其为 Tier-2 风险
